ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #12, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-25-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Logan for and with Mark L. Cederborg. Hana Totah (Beneficiary) appeared in pro per.

Mr. Logan stated that some of the fees were paid before court approval and that by paying some of the fees in 2008 that it was a benefit to the trust. The Court stated that the CMC statement needed to be scrutinized a little more. Mr. Logan stated that each beneficiary was to receive $62,000.00, that not only did he pay the fees but that he distributed $15,000.00 to each of the seven beneficiaries in an attempt to lower the tax liability. Ms. Totah stated that she only received about $14,000.00 and noted that there was also rental income. Mr. Logan presented argument in support of the fees requested noting that this matter has become somewhat time consuming. He further stated that this was scheduled as the twelfth and final account, that he may come in with a supplemental account, and that it will be set in the normal course. In addition, he noted that each beneficiary would be receiving approximately $50,000.00 and that he would explain it to Hana Totah. At the conclusion of counsel's statements, the Court took the matter under submission.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Logan for Mark Cederborg (Trustee). Henry Totah and Raymond Totah were also present. Hanna Totah in pro per.

Raymond Totah stated the their attorney, George Engler, had another matter and that he would not be appearing. The Court stated that there were two petitions on calendar: Hanna Totah's petiton for distribution and the eighth account and report by Mr. Cederborg. The Court also stated that a filing came in Friday which cleared the eighth account, that there was no opposition to the eighth account, and that it will want a ninth account to be filed. Mr. Cederborg stated that the ninth account should run through December 2009. Raymond Totah stated that Mr. Engler wanted back up materials and checks in regard to the eighth account. The Court stated that Mr. Engler could not get the back up material that he requested, and that it was signing the order as to the eighth account.
